# Shell fluency — from lost at the prompt to sculpting text 🟢

**Goal.** Find your feet at the shell, learn to program it, then wield grep/sed/awk on real text. The primary track is Matt Might's (he names the survival guide as the prerequisite to bash-by-example); an equivalent Carpentries + Robbins track is a side-quest.

**Audience:** complete beginners welcome  
**Effort:** ⏱ ~1–2h  
**Prerequisites:** none

| # | Lab | You'll learn | Checkpoint (observable) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [`UNIX_novice_survival_guide`](../UNIX_novice_survival_guide/) | navigate, pipe, redirect, and get help without a cheat-sheet. | in the `~/unix-survival` sandbox, reproduce the guide's `find \| grep READ` and rename `baz`→`bar`, unaided. |
| 2 | [`shell-intermediate-programming-by-example`](../shell-intermediate-programming-by-example/) | variables, loops, functions, arguments — programming the shell, not just typing at it. | run `demo.sh` → it prints `5!=120`; then write your own script with a loop + a function that processes argv. |
| 3 | [`UNIX-sculpting-text-regex-grep-sed-awk`](../UNIX-sculpting-text-regex-grep-sed-awk/) | wield the GNU grep/sed/awk trio on real text. | run `demo.sh` (byte-identical on Debian + Alpine); then extract and reformat fields from a log in a single `awk` line. |

### Optional side-quests

- [`shell-novice-workshop`](../shell-novice-workshop/) — the Carpentries (CC-BY) full-day equivalent of steps 1–2, by different authors.
- [`shell-intermediate-workshop`](../shell-intermediate-workshop/) — Robbins' 'Bash by example' — the Carpentries track's programming follow-on.
